title: ZimerfeldLFS — Git Large File Storage
notARepo: The selected folder is not a Git repository.
aboutText:
ZimerfeldLFS

Git Large File Storage (LFS) replaces large files (audio, video, datasets) with lightweight text pointers inside your repository. The actual file content is hosted on a separate remote server, speeding up cloning and preventing repository bloat.

This window guides you through three steps:
  1. Installation — git lfs install
  2. Basic workflow — track patterns, commit, push
  3. Cloning & pulling — git lfs pull / fetch / checkout

Copyright © 2026 Zimerfeld — CC BY-NC-ND 4.0
step1Help:
On Windows & macOS, Git LFS is typically included out of the box.
If you need to install it manually, use Homebrew (brew install git-lfs), Chocolatey (choco install git-lfs) or the official binaries from https://git-lfs.com.

Then initialize LFS for your user account by clicking "git lfs install" below (runs once per machine).
installMissing: Git LFS is NOT installed or not on PATH.
installReady: Git LFS ready — {0} (initialized for this user).
installAvailable: Git LFS available — {0}. Click "git lfs install" to initialize it.
trackHint: Tell Git LFS which file types to manage using glob patterns (e.g. *.psd, *.mp4, *.zip).
step3Help:
When collaborators or deployment tools clone the repository, Git LFS downloads the heavy files automatically as they check out the branch.

To fetch or restore LFS content later, use the buttons below:
  • git lfs pull — download LFS content for the current checkout
  • git lfs fetch --all — prefetch LFS objects for every ref
  • git lfs checkout — populate working-tree files from downloaded objects
  • git lfs status — show the status of LFS objects
logCommitUnavailable: Commit dialog unavailable in this context.
logPushFallback: Host push dialog unavailable — running plain "git push".
errEmptyPattern: Enter a glob pattern to track (e.g. *.psd).
errNoPatternSelected: Select a tracked pattern to untrack.
errNoRepo: Select a valid Git repository first.
